title: Roguelite Persistence & Graveyard
problem: On death the entire run resets — loot, gear, progress. This punishes investment in the loot system which is the core of the game. Need a death model that keeps equipped gear across runs but still has real stakes.
analysis: gameStore.endRun(false) handles defeat. inventoryStore holds bag (run-scoped) and equipped (cross-run). The bag is run-scoped — cleared on death. Equipped items carry across runs, but one is sacrificed each death. The graveyard is a permanent session record (Zustand in-memory, persisted via AsyncStorage save).
implementation: 1. Add GraveyardEntry type: { item: Item, floor: number, tier: number, level: number, lostAt: string (ISO date), classId?: ClassId | null, killedBy?: string }. 2. gameStore: add graveyard: GraveyardEntry[] and lastSacrifice: GraveyardEntry | null. 3. endRun(false): (a) pick 1 quality-weighted item from EQUIPPED ITEMS ONLY → graveyard; (b) clear entire bag (createInventory()); (c) non-sacrificed equipped items survive. 4. On new run start (ClassSelectScreen doDescend): refillPotions() clears any leftover potions (bag already empty), then adds 3 HP + 1 mana potion. 5. GraveyardScreen: ScrollView of GraveyardEntry cards. 6. GameOver screen: shows sacrificed item name. 7. Ghost Echo: GraveyardScreen exposes invokeGhostEcho(entryIndex) — sets ghostCharm: entry.item in gameStore. When active, GridScreen reads ghostCharm and passes it to combatStore; the charm grants 25% of the item's stats for 1 floor, then clears on nextFloor(). 8. Ghost Echo ambient hint: shown in GridScreen after 2 seconds when graveyard.length > 0 and no ghostCharm is active and hasSeenGhostEchoHint is false (settingsStore). Hint auto-dismisses after 4 seconds and calls markGhostEchoHintSeen() so it only ever shows once.

decisions.decision: Career stats persisted across runs via AsyncStorage (save/load in persistence.ts)
decisions.rationale: Career stats are cross-run motivators — they must survive app restarts to have meaning.

decisions.decision: Graveyard stores items lost on death for Ghost Echo mechanic — invokeGhostEcho is fully live
decisions.rationale: Graveyard data structure was built first; Ghost Echo activation (invokeGhostEcho, ghostCharm, 25% stat pass-through for 1 floor) shipped in a subsequent session and is fully wired in GridScreen and combatStore.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: dailyStreak + lastRunDate added to CareerStats — ISO date comparison in endRun
decisions.rationale: Daily streak incentivises return visits; ISO YYYY-MM-DD string comparison is simple and timezone-stable for the common case.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: streak resets to 1 on a missed day, increments if played on consecutive days, shown as fire emoji on MainMenu when streak is 2 or more
decisions.rationale: Threshold of 2 avoids showing the streak indicator on day 1 of a new streak where it carries no meaning.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: runKills and runItemsFound track per-run stats, reset on startRun, shown in EndScreen run summary card
decisions.rationale: Per-run stats give players a sense of accomplishment even on a losing run; reset on startRun ensures no carry-over.

decisions.decision: BMAD S3: lastStandUsed boolean added to GameState — single death-defiance per run, survives at 1 HP, fires in both attackAction and skillAction defeat paths
decisions.rationale: Single-use keeps it rare and meaningful. Covering both defeat paths (attack and skill) ensures no edge case skips it.

successCriteria: Death with equipped items always produces 1 graveyard entry (from equipped pool). Bag is always fully cleared on death regardless of contents. No equipped item other than the sacrifice is removed. Next run always starts with 3 HP + 1 mana potion (doDescend). Graveyard persists across app restarts. GameOver screen names the sacrificed item. Death with no equipped items clears bag and produces no graveyard entry.
knownLimitations: Ghost Echo mechanic (use graveyard items as one-floor power) is UI note only — not implemented
Daily streak uses UTC dates — players crossing midnight in non-UTC timezone may see unexpected streak breaks
recommendations: Use @react-native-async-storage/async-storage (already a dep). Graveyard entries should be append-only — never mutate past entries. The shared stash is the only way to protect items across death — this is an intentional design pressure.

revisions.author: claude

publicInterface.name: GraveyardEntry
publicInterface.description: { item: Item, floor: number, tier: number, level: number, lostAt: string, classId?: ClassId | null, killedBy?: string }

publicInterface.name: GraveyardScreen
publicInterface.description: Full-screen list of all items lost to death, accessible from MainMenu and post-death EndScreen. Tap an entry to invoke Ghost Echo.

publicInterface.name: invokeGhostEcho
publicInterface.description: Sets ghostCharm to the graveyard entry's item — grants 25% stats for 1 floor. No-ops if echo already active.

publicInterface.name: ghostCharm
publicInterface.description: Active Ghost Echo item; cleared by nextFloor() and clearGhostCharm()

codeSnippets.label: Quality-weighted sacrifice from equipped items only
Atomic bag wipe + equipped sacrifice in one setState
Graveyard entry with killedBy
Ghost Echo invocation (gameStore)
Ghost Echo ambient hint (GridScreen)

userGuide:
Your equipped gear survives death — but fate claims one piece as tribute. The sacrifice is drawn from your equipped items only, quality-weighted (a Unique is 40× more likely to be taken than a Normal). Your bag is always wiped on death — run loot does not carry forward.

On your next run you start with exactly 3 HP potions and 1 mana potion.

**Important:** Items in the shared stash are NEVER at risk — only equipped items are in the sacrifice pool. If you want to protect a piece of gear, deposit it to the stash in town before a dangerous fight.

If you die with no equipped items, no graveyard entry is created (bag is still wiped).

**Ghost Echo:** Open the Graveyard screen (accessible from the main menu and the post-death screen) and tap any entry to invoke that item as a Ghost Echo. You gain 25% of its stats for exactly 1 floor — after descending or dying, the echo fades. Only one echo can be active at a time. A one-time ambient hint appears on the dungeon grid to help you discover this mechanic.

codexEntry.title: Death & the Graveyard
codexEntry.summary: Your equipped gear survives death — except one piece. Your bag is always wiped. Start the next run fresh.
mechanics: On death: entire bag is wiped — run loot is gone
On death: one equipped item is sacrificed to the Graveyard
Sacrifice is drawn from equipped items only (not bag)
Sacrifice probability is quality-weighted: Unique = 40pts, Rare = 20pts, Magic = 5pts, Normal = 1pt
A Unique item is ~40× more likely to be sacrificed than a Normal item
Non-sacrificed equipped items survive death intact
Next run always starts with 3 HP potions + 1 mana potion
Shared stash items are never at risk
Floor resets to 1 on death. Tier and level are never reduced.
tips: Stash your best-in-slot gear before a dangerous fight to protect it from sacrifice.
The bag is always cleared on death — don't hoard run loot you cannot afford to lose.
The Graveyard is accessible from the main menu. Each entry shows the floor, tier, class, and date of loss.

statusHistory.reason: Death mechanic redesign: bag always wiped on death, sacrifice pool changed to equipped-only

BMAD session 3: added daily streak tracking (dailyStreak+lastRunDate in CareerStats), per-run kill/item counters (runKills/runItemsFound), Last Stand single death-defiance per run (lastStandUsed flag), EndScreen run summary card
